description: Workflow is the Schema for the Workflows API.

apiVersion.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources

kind.description:
Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.
Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.
Cannot be updated.
In CamelCase.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds

spec.description: WorkflowSpec defines the desired state of Workflow.

bootOptions.description:
BootOptions are options that control the booting of Hardware.
These are only applicable when a HardwareRef is provided.

bootMode.description: BootMode is the type of booting that will be done. One of "netboot", "isoboot", or "customboot".

custombootConfig.description:
CustombootConfig is the configuration for the "customboot" boot mode.
This allows users to define custom BMC Actions.

postActions.description:
PostActions are the BMC Actions that will be run after all Workflow Actions have completed.
In most cases these Actions should get a Machine into a state where it can be powered off or rebooted and remove any mounted virtual media.
These Actions will be run only if the main Workflow Actions complete successfully.

items.description:
Action represents the action to be performed.
A single task can only perform one type of action.
For example either PowerAction or OneTimeBootDeviceAction.

bootDevice.description: BootDevice is the device to set as the first boot device on the Machine.

device.description: Device is the name of the device to set as the first boot device.

efiBoot.description: EFIBoot indicates whether the boot device should be set to efiboot mode.

persistent.description: Persistent indicates whether the boot device should be set persistently as the first boot device.

oneTimeBootDeviceAction.description:
OneTimeBootDeviceAction represents a baseboard management one time set boot device operation.

Deprecated: This field is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Use bootDevice instead.

device.description:
Devices represents the boot devices, in order for setting one time boot.
Currently only the first device in the slice is used to set one time boot.

items.description: BootDevice represents boot device of the Machine.

efiBoot.description: EFIBoot instructs the machine to use EFI boot.

powerAction.description: PowerAction represents a baseboard management power operation.

virtualMediaAction.description: VirtualMediaAction represents a baseboard management virtual media insert/eject.

kind.description: Kind represents the kind of virtual media.

mediaURL.description: mediaURL represents the URL of the image to be inserted into the virtual media, or empty to eject media.

preparingActions.description:
PreparingActions are the BMC Actions that will be run before any Workflow Actions.
In most cases these Actions should get a Machine into a state where a Tink Agent is running.

items.description:
Action represents the action to be performed.
A single task can only perform one type of action.
For example either PowerAction or OneTimeBootDeviceAction.

bootDevice.description: BootDevice is the device to set as the first boot device on the Machine.

device.description: Device is the name of the device to set as the first boot device.

efiBoot.description: EFIBoot indicates whether the boot device should be set to efiboot mode.

persistent.description: Persistent indicates whether the boot device should be set persistently as the first boot device.

oneTimeBootDeviceAction.description:
OneTimeBootDeviceAction represents a baseboard management one time set boot device operation.

Deprecated: This field is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Use bootDevice instead.

device.description:
Devices represents the boot devices, in order for setting one time boot.
Currently only the first device in the slice is used to set one time boot.

items.description: BootDevice represents boot device of the Machine.

efiBoot.description: EFIBoot instructs the machine to use EFI boot.

powerAction.description: PowerAction represents a baseboard management power operation.

virtualMediaAction.description: VirtualMediaAction represents a baseboard management virtual media insert/eject.

kind.description: Kind represents the kind of virtual media.

mediaURL.description: mediaURL represents the URL of the image to be inserted into the virtual media, or empty to eject media.

isoURL.description:
ISOURL is the URL of the ISO that will be one-time booted. When this field is set,
the controller will create a job.bmc.tinkerbell.org object
for getting the associated hardware into a CDROM booting state.
A HardwareRef that contains a spec.BmcRef must be provided.
BootMode must be set to "isoboot".

toggleAllowNetboot.description:
ToggleAllowNetboot indicates whether the controller should toggle the field in the associated hardware for allowing PXE booting.
This will be enabled before a Workflow is executed and disabled after the Workflow has completed successfully.
A HardwareRef must be provided.

disabled.description: Disabled indicates whether the Workflow will be processed or not.

hardwareMap.description: A mapping of template devices to hardware mac addresses.

hardwareRef.description: Name of the Hardware associated with this workflow.

templateRef.description: Name of the Template associated with this workflow.

status.description: WorkflowStatus defines the observed state of a Workflow.

agentID.description: AgentID is the ID of the Agent with which this Workflow is associated.

bootOptions.description: BootOptions holds the state of any boot options.

allowNetboot.description: AllowNetboot holds the state of the the controller's interactions with the allowPXE field in a Hardware object.

jobs.description: Jobs holds the state of any job.bmc.tinkerbell.org objects created.

additionalProperties.description: JobStatus holds the state of a specific job.bmc.tinkerbell.org object created.

complete.description: Complete indicates whether the created job.bmc.tinkerbell.org has reported its conditions as complete.

existingJobDeleted.description:
ExistingJobDeleted indicates whether any existing job.bmc.tinkerbell.org was deleted.
The name of each job.bmc.tinkerbell.org object created by the controller is the same, so only one can exist at a time.
Using the same name was chosen so that there is only ever 1 job.bmc.tinkerbell.org per Hardware/Machine.bmc.tinkerbell.org.
This makes clean up easier and we dont just orphan jobs every time.

uid.description:
UID is the UID of the job.bmc.tinkerbell.org object associated with this workflow.
This is used to uniquely identify the job.bmc.tinkerbell.org object, as
all objects for a specific Hardware/Machine.bmc.tinkerbell.org are created with the same name.

conditions.description: Conditions are the latest available observations of an object's current state.

items.description: JobCondition describes current state of a job.

message.description: Message is a human readable message indicating details about last transition.

reason.description: Reason is a (brief) reason for the condition's last transition.

status.description: Status of the condition, one of True, False, Unknown.

time.description: Time when the condition was created.

type.description: Type of job condition, Complete or Failed.

currentState.description: CurrentState tracks where the workflow is in its execution.

globalExecutionStop.description:
GlobalExecutionStop represents the time when the Workflow should stop executing.
After this time, the Workflow will be marked as TIMEOUT.

globalTimeout.description: GlobalTimeout represents the max execution time.

state.description: State is the current overall state of the Workflow.

tasks.description: Tasks are the tasks to be run by the Agent(s).

items.description: Task represents a series of actions to be completed by an Agent.

templateRendering.description:
TemplateRendering indicates whether the template was rendered successfully.
Possible values are "successful" or "failed" or "unknown".
